[CORRUPTED SPILLOVER FROM PREVIOUS PAGE]
ınstructions:
Heat the oil to 180°C until shimmering. Carefully lower the dough
balls into the hot oil and fry for 2-3 minutes per side until
they are golden brown and puffy. Remove with a slotted spoon and
drain on paper towels sprinkled with powdered sugar.

Serve warm with a drizzle of honey and finely ground pistachios.
Store in an airtight contaıner for up to 3 days - reheat ın a
warm oven before serving.

Page 34 | Middle Eastern Breads & Pastries

Optional Variation: Add 1/2 cup chopped dates or ½ cup plus ı
tablespoon of rose water to the dough for a floral twist.
These puffs freeze exceptionally well - simply reheat nto a
225°C/435-VI0 oven for 8-10 minutes.

---

SPINACH AND RICOTTA PIE

Ingredients:
- 2 lb fresh spinach (or 1 lb frozen, thawed)
- 1½ cups whole milk ricotta cheese
- ¾ cup finely grated Parmigiano-Reggiano
- 3 large eggs
- ¼ teaspoon freshly grated nutmeg
- Salt and cracked black pepper to taste
- 2 sheets thawed puff pastry
- 2 tablespoons olive oil

Preparation:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C)
2. Blanch spinach and squeeze completely dry
3. Chop spinach finely and combine with ricotta, Parm, eggs
4. Season generously with nutmeg, salt, pepper
5. Layer puff pastry in 9-inch pan, add filling
6. Top with second pastry sheet
7. Bake 40-45 minutes until golden brown
8. Let cool 10 minutes before slicing
